title: Signal design for quality screening
prompt: An intermediary commits to a two-message signal policy based on a noisy quality test. Receivers decide whether to transact after seeing the message. The intermediary maximizes expected completed transactions, not receiver surplus or accuracy. Frame the information-design problem and its limits.

model_specification.mechanism: Disclosure changes posterior beliefs and therefore receivers' transaction decisions.

prohibited_inferences: More transactions imply more accurate beliefs or greater welfare
